title: Suricata IDS Alert Volume Spike per Sensor (Signature Storm or Blind Spot Detection)
dataSources: Suricata EVE `alert` events (`sourcetype=suricata:alert` or `suricata:event`); Snort `alert` events (`sourcetype=snort` per TA)
spl:
index=ids (sourcetype="suricata:alert" OR sourcetype="suricata:event" OR sourcetype="snort") earliest=-48h
| eval sensor=coalesce(sensor_name, host, hostname)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, sensor
| eventstats avg(count) as mu, stdev(count) as sigma by sensor
| where count > mu+4*sigma AND count > 100
| sort -count
description: A sudden order-of-magnitude increase in alerts from one sensor usually means a new noisy rule was enabled, a mirror or tap failed asymmetrically, or a worm is spreading inside a VLAN. Conversely, a flatline may mean the sensor stopped seeing traffic. Both conditions threaten detection SLAs.
value: Gives SOC managers an operational control metric that is independent of whether individual signatures are true positives.
implementation: Standardise `sensor` naming in `inputs.conf` or heavy-forwarder metadata. Lower the `count > 100` floor on lab sensors. When alerts fire, open a bridge with NetOps to validate SPAN health. Pair with `suricata:stats` if exported for dropped-packet visibility.
visualization: Time chart (alerts per sensor), Table (sensor, z-score), Single value (sensors breaching threshold).
